skill.name: gws-forms
skill.description: Manage Google Forms through the gws CLI. Create, read, update forms and manage responses and webhooks.
skill.summary: Manage Google Forms via the gws CLI tool
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All 18 static findings are false positives. The skill documents gws CLI commands in markdown code blocks. No actual shell execution, path traversal vulnerabilities, or cryptographic algorithms are present. This is a legitimate Google Workspace CLI wrapper for Google Forms API.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks as Ruby backtick execution. These are fenced code blocks demonstrating gws CLI usage, not actual code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Fenced code blocks (```bash) are documentation, not execution. The analyzer misidentified markdown syntax as shell backticks.
medium_findings.reasoning: The skill file is markdown documentation showing gws CLI commands. No actual shell execution occurs - these are examples for Claude to read and understand the CLI interface.

medium_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '../gws-shared/SKILL.md' as path traversal. This is a valid relative path reference to another skill file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reference to a sibling skill file is a standard pattern in this codebase, not a path traversal vulnerability.
medium_findings.reasoning: The path '../gws-shared/SKILL.md' references the authentication skill documentation. This is a standard skill documentation pattern, not a vulnerability.

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the word 'MD5' in form field type documentation. No cryptographic algorithms are actually used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 appears in form field type documentation, not as a cryptographic implementation. No actual crypto usage in this skill.
medium_findings.reasoning: MD5 is mentioned as a Google Forms field type. This skill does not implement or use any cryptographic algorithms.

content.user_title: Automate Google Forms workflows with Claude
content.value_statement: Manually managing Google Forms responses and settings is time-consuming. This skill enables Claude to create forms, retrieve responses, and update settings through a simple CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Create new Google Forms with custom titles
Batch update form properties and structure
Access form responses and response data
Set up webhook watches for form events
limitations: Requires gws CLI tool installed and configured
Cannot create form items directly after form creation (requires separate update call)
Authentication setup via gws-shared skill is required before use
Legacy forms lack publish_settings field support

use_cases.title: Automated Survey Creation
use_cases.description: Create new employee feedback forms or customer surveys on demand with dynamic titles and settings.

use_cases.title: Form Response Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up webhook notifications to track new form submissions in real-time for integration with other systems.

use_cases.title: Bulk Form Updates
use_cases.description: Update settings or properties across multiple Google Forms in batch operations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new form
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google Form titled '[Form Title]' using the gws forms CLI
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to create a new survey or questionnaire

prompt_templates.title: Get form responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all responses from the Google Form at [form-id]
prompt_templates.scenario: When retrieving submission data from a form

prompt_templates.title: Update form settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the publish settings for form [form-id] to allow anyone to respond

prompt_templates.title: Batch update forms
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a batch update on form [form-id] with the following changes: [description]
prompt_templates.scenario: When making multiple changes to a form structure

output_examples.input: Create a new form titled 'Team Feedback Survey'
output_examples.output: Created form with ID: 1ABC234def456... You can view it at: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1ABC234def456/edit

Form: Team Feedback Survey
Responses: 42 total
Last submission: 2026-04-24

Latest: 'The new workflow has improved our sprint planning significantly.'

best_practices: Always inspect the schema with 'gws schema forms.<resource>.<method>' before calling API methods to understand required parameters
Set up webhook watches for forms that need real-time response tracking to avoid polling
Read the gws-shared/SKILL.md file first to understand authentication and security configuration
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to create forms with items in the initial create call - items must be added via separate update calls
Do not ignore prerequisite requirements - gws-shared documentation must be read first
Do not use this skill with legacy Google Forms that do not support publish_settings

faq.question: What is required before using this skill?
faq.answer: You must read the gws-shared/SKILL.md file first for authentication setup. The gws CLI tool must also be installed and configured with Google credentials.

faq.question: Can I create a form with questions in one step?
faq.answer: No. Google Forms API only copies the title from the initial create request. You must first create an empty form, then use forms.batchUpdate to add items.

faq.question: How do I monitor new form responses?
faq.answer: Use the 'watches' resource to set up webhook notifications. This allows your system to receive real-time alerts when someone submits a response.

faq.question: What are the available API methods?
faq.answer: Available methods are: batchUpdate, create, get, setPublishSettings, responses, and watches. Run 'gws forms --help' to see all options.

faq.question: Can I use this with legacy Google Forms?
faq.answer: Legacy forms do not support the setPublishSettings method because they lack the publish_settings field. Use get and responses operations instead.

faq.question: How do I find the correct parameters for a method?
faq.answer: Use 'gws schema forms.<resource>.<method>' to inspect the required parameters, types, and default values for any API method.
